title: HIP IPC Multiprocess Shared Buffer Workflow

problem.summary: Template a producer/consumer HIP IPC workflow with exported memory handles, event handles, optional P2P access, and explicit synchronization boundaries.
inputs: consumer process stream and device ordinal
outputs: shared device buffer observed by the consumer process
IPC setup, transfer, and kernel timing records
constraints: Template-only; no IPC latency, P2P bandwidth, or counter evidence is claimed
Record process topology, device ordinals, peer-access status, handle lifetime, and synchronization contract
Separate handle setup cost from steady-state cross-process work

verification.method: Producer fills a shared device buffer, consumer opens the IPC handle, runs a verification kernel, and compares a checksum against host-side expected values.

curation.notes: ROCm parity port from CUDA task `cuda-ipc-multiprocess`. This is a template/harness scaffold only; CUDA-origin result artifacts were intentionally not copied, and no ROCm timing, correctness, or profiler-counter evidence is claimed yet. Run on AMD hardware, attach ROCm version, hipcc flags, gfx target, and label HIP-event-only results as timing-only.
